text.title: FinOps — Cloud Cost Allocation via Tagging Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the fco-cost-allocation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every taggable resource MUST carry a mandatory tag set: `team`, `service`, `environment`, `cost-center`, `owner-email`. Resources missing tags MUST fail creation.

rationale.source: AWS Tag Policies docs, FinOps Foundation tagging guide

rule.rationale: Optional tags = no tags. Allocation only works if tag coverage is 100% at creation time.

rule.statement: Tag enforcement MUST be at creation time (SCP / Organization Policy / Azure Policy `deny` effect); ex-post tagging campaigns are forbidden as primary mechanism.

rationale.source: AWS SCP docs, GCP Org Policy docs

rule.rationale: Ex-post tagging campaigns achieve 60-80% coverage at best; creation-time enforcement is the only path to 100%.

rule.statement: Tag values for `team` / `environment` / `cost-center` MUST come from a controlled vocabulary (e.g. `prod|staging|dev`); free-text is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Free-text values produce `prod`/`Prod`/`production`/`prd` chaos; allocation reports cannot reconcile.

rule.statement: An automated job MUST run daily, listing untagged or wrong-tagged resources; report MUST notify the offending account owner.

rationale.source: AWS Config rules, GCP Asset Inventory

rule.rationale: Even with SCPs, edge cases slip through (legacy resources, console exceptions). The daily report closes the loop.

rule.statement: The chosen tag keys MUST be marked as 'cost allocation tags' in billing / cost-explorer so they appear in chargeback reports.
rule.rationale: A tag not activated for cost allocation does not appear in CUR / BigQuery billing export — allocation is invisible.

rule.statement: If the account is single-team or spend < $5k/mo, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Tag policy overhead is justified by allocation need; small accounts can settle for account-level attribution.
